trajectory.description: Starting fresh. I'll implement a DenseNet-121 encoder + Transformer decoder for HMER (Handwritten Math Expression Recognition). The approach: 1) DenseNet-121 feature extractor, 2) 2D positional encoding, 3) Transformer decoder with attention. I'll train for a few epochs first to validate the pipeline and measure iteration time.

trajectory.description:
Observation: The PositionalEncoding2D class tries to register a tuple as a buffer, which fails. The `_build_pe` returns a tuple `(pe_h, pe_w)` but `register_buffer` expects a single tensor. I need to fix this by registering them separately. Also need to fix the forward method accordingly.

Analysis: Simple bug - register two separate buffers instead of a tuple.

Decision: Fix the model.py bug and run training for 5 epochs to validate pipeline.

trajectory.description: Observation: After 54 epochs, val exprate=0.0071, loss=2.14. The model is barely learning. Analysis: The DenseNet-121 with stride 32 gives only 4x16=64 spatial positions from 128x512 input - too few. Also the model may be too small. The loss is still very high. Decision: Redesign with (1) better encoder with less downsampling - use DenseNet features but with smaller stride, (2) larger model (d_model=512, 6 decoder layers), (3) better LR with warmup, (4) larger batch size. Also fix the image preprocessing to use a more appropriate size.

Observation: After 54 epochs, val exprate=0.0071. The model is barely learning. The DenseNet encoder has a bug - norm5 is after denseblock4 but we're using it after denseblock3. Also the model is too small. Analysis: Need to redesign with (1) proper encoder - use full DenseNet but with stride reduction by removing pool0 or modifying strides, (2) larger model d_model=512, 6 decoder layers, (3) better training. Decision: Completely redesign the model with a proper DenseNet encoder (use features up to denseblock3 without the buggy norm5), larger decoder, and better training setup.

trajectory.description: Observation: DenseNet encoder fails because denseblock3 outputs 1024 channels, not 512. DenseNet-121: denseblock1→256ch, transition1→128ch, denseblock2→512ch, transition2→256ch, denseblock3→1024ch. Analysis: Simple channel count bug. Also the model was barely learning after 54 epochs - need better training. Decision: Fix the encoder (1024 channels), also improve the training with better LR schedule and data augmentation. The architecture is otherwise sound - 29M params, d_model=512, 6 decoder layers.

trajectory.description: Observation: Val exprate plateaued at 0.1481 after ~80 epochs. Loss declining slowly but exprate not improving. Analysis: The model is stuck. Need to train from scratch with better hyperparameters - lower dropout (0.3 is too high for this task), better LR schedule (cosine), and potentially larger batch. The current approach of resuming with reduced LR isn't working well. Decision: Train from scratch with dropout=0.1, cosine LR schedule, batch_size=48, 100 epochs. This should give a much better result.
